question.text: As per Regulation 2(f) of SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015, 'immediate relative' includes which of the following?

answer.text: Only the spouse of the person
All blood relatives up to second degree
Spouse, parent, sibling, and child of such person or of the spouse, who is financially dependent or consults the person in trading decisions
Only persons living in the same household

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 2(f) defines immediate relative as spouse, and includes parent, sibling, and child of such person or of the spouse, provided any of whom is either financially dependent or consults the person in taking trading decisions. **The Trap:** The definition is not based solely on blood relationship - it requires financial dependence or consultation in trading decisions. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) also covers the concept of immediate relatives for disclosure purposes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The two conditions - financial dependence OR consultation in trading decisions - are alternative, not cumulative.
